rows.name: Low Tiger Shot
rows.notes: Fast 2-hit OD projectile; can hit crouching opponents; can be used while a meterless Tiger Shot is still on-screen; the projectile travels low to the ground, preventing many low profile attacks from avoiding it (e.g. Kimberly's slide, A.K.I.'s Sinister Slide); Sagat is in a crouching state for the entire move duration with crouch-sized hurtboxes; puts opponents into a limited juggle state; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 16-37 and a leg hurtbox on frames 12-44; topmost hurtboxes are vulnerable only to air attacks on frames 1-44 (more vulnerable to jump-ins despite crouching) and are projectile invincible on frames 1-29 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 30-44 are still vulnerable); starter scaling does not apply if only the 2nd hit connects (e.g. clashing with 1-hit OD fireball or breaking Drive Impact armor)

raw.notes: Fast 2-hit OD projectile; can hit crouching opponents; can be used while a meterless Tiger Shot is still on-screen; the projectile travels low to the ground, preventing many low profile attacks from avoiding it (e.g. Kimberly's slide, A.K.I.'s Sinister Slide); Sagat is in a crouching state for the entire move duration with crouch-sized hurtboxes; puts opponents into a limited juggle state; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 16-37 and a leg hurtbox on frames 12-44; topmost hurtboxes are vulnerable only to air attacks on frames 1-44 (more vulnerable to jump-ins despite crouching) and are projectile invincible on frames 1-29 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 30-44 are still vulnerable); starter scaling does not apply if only the 2nd hit connects (e.g. clashing with 1-hit OD fireball or breaking Drive Impact armor)

rows.name: Tiger Uppercut (hold)
rows.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_5.png
rows.notes: Button must be held 24f for fully charged version; causes a wall splat on hit that leaves Sagat at a fixed distance (no follow-up combo); deals 1100 less damage on active frames 4-10 with no wallsplat animation (KD +29 on active frame 4); cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-3 only)

raw.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_5.png
raw.notes: Button must be held 24f for fully charged version; causes a wall splat on hit that leaves Sagat at a fixed distance (no follow-up combo); deals 1100 less damage on active frames 4-10 with no wallsplat animation (KD +29 on active frame 4); cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-3 only)

rows.name: Mighty Tiger
rows.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps (unless the 6LK input is delayed from 214LK/MK); puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after Counter-hit 214LK/MK); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; loses to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214LK/MK (but safe after 214HK unless the 6LK input is delayed); if used from a 2-light chain, opponent is in range for Throw or 2LK~5LP chain (too far if starting with 3-light chain)

raw.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps (unless the 6LK input is delayed from 214LK/MK); puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after Counter-hit 214LK/MK); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; loses to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214LK/MK (but safe after 214HK unless the 6LK input is delayed); if used from a 2-light chain, opponent is in range for Throw or 2LK~5LP chain (too far if starting with 3-light chain)

rows.name: Mighty Tiger
rows.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps, making it a weak option; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after DI wallsplat juggle); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK unless the 6LK input is delayed

raw.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps, making it a weak option; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after DI wallsplat juggle); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK unless the 6LK input is delayed

rows.name: Greedy Tiger
rows.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner, or 623MP from CH/PC 214LK/MK launch); frame data vs. crouch from 214LK/214MK: -19 oH / -26 oB / KD +51 onPC launch; frame data vs. crouch from 214HK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC (214HK keeps opponent crouching on Punish Counter); safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214K; 5f blockstring gap after 214LK/MK (loses to reversal standing/crouching 4f normals); 3f blockstring gap after 214HK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

raw.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner, or 623MP from CH/PC 214LK/MK launch); frame data vs. crouch from 214LK/214MK: -19 oH / -26 oB / KD +51 onPC launch; frame data vs. crouch from 214HK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC (214HK keeps opponent crouching on Punish Counter); safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214K; 5f blockstring gap after 214LK/MK (loses to reversal standing/crouching 4f normals); 3f blockstring gap after 214HK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

rows.name: Greedy Tiger
rows.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner); frame data vs. crouch from 214KK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK; 3f blockstring gap after 214KK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

raw.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner); frame data vs. crouch from 214KK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK; 3f blockstring gap after 214KK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

rows.name: Nova Tiger
rows.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; only combos naturally from 214HK (requires juggle state for LK/MK versions, like a CH/PC Tiger Nexus); after blocked 214LK/MK, interruptible 9f blockstring gap (5f gap vs. Burnout, loses to 4f normals); after blocked 214HK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214HK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

raw.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; only combos naturally from 214HK (requires juggle state for LK/MK versions, like a CH/PC Tiger Nexus); after blocked 214LK/MK, interruptible 9f blockstring gap (5f gap vs. Burnout, loses to 4f normals); after blocked 214HK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214HK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

rows.name: Nova Tiger
rows.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; combos naturally from 214KK; after blocked 214KK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214KK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

raw.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; combos naturally from 214KK; after blocked 214KK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214KK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

rows.name:
Savage Tiger Raid
(Damage)
rows.notes: Highest damage variant of SA2; leaves the opponent far away with no oki; has significant corner carry (over 1/2 screen)

raw.notes: Highest damage variant of SA2; leaves the opponent far away with no oki; has significant corner carry (over 1/2 screen)

rows.name:
Savage Tiger Zenith
(Launcher)
rows.notes: Launches the opponent into a limited juggle state (juggle counter is always set to 2 regardless of previous juggle state); Hard Knockdown if the opponent hits the ground instead of being juggled; useful for corner carry, building back some Super gauge, and getting a good mix of damage + oki (but usually results in less damage than Tiger Raid)

raw.notes: Launches the opponent into a limited juggle state (juggle counter is always set to 2 regardless of previous juggle state); Hard Knockdown if the opponent hits the ground instead of being juggled; useful for corner carry, building back some Super gauge, and getting a good mix of damage + oki (but usually results in less damage than Tiger Raid)

rows.name:
Savage Tiger Pendulum
(Side Switch)
rows.notes: Switches sides with opponent; leaves the opponent far away with no oki

raw.notes: Switches sides with opponent; leaves the opponent far away with no oki

rows.name:
Savage Tiger Stomp
(Oki)
rows.notes: Leaves the opponent close to Sagat for stronger oki than other versions; can walk up for a strike/throw/shimmy mixup; gives enough advantage for an immediate 6MP overhead

raw.notes: Leaves the opponent close to Sagat for stronger oki than other versions; can walk up for a strike/throw/shimmy mixup; gives enough advantage for an immediate 6MP overhead

rows.name: Neutral Taunt
rows.notes: "If you desire the throne, stand tall against the emperor."

raw.notes: "If you desire the throne, stand tall against the emperor."

rows.name: Forward Taunt
rows.notes: "The emperor's strength is peerless. However, it would be my pleasure to test your strength!"

raw.notes: "The emperor's strength is peerless. However, it would be my pleasure to test your strength!"
